fresh_source_discovery.route: Candy Cane Park / Dyersville to Rockville Road
fresh_source_discovery.blocker: Wisconsin River Trips documents this fresh 6.6-mile Dyersville route and publishes a useful same-river Fulton-gauge ladder, but the take-out is a muddy Rockville Road bridge access with no public landing or land-manager confirmation found in this pass. The public endpoint bar is not met until Rockville Road is confirmed as legal/practical public access or a cleaner downstream named take-out is identified.
fresh_source_discovery.retryCondition: Retry only if Dyersville, Dubuque County, Iowa DNR, or a water-trail manager confirms Rockville Road as public canoe/kayak access, if a cleaner named downstream public take-out appears, or if the direct USGS 05418100 Dyersville site gains current discharge support and a matching route-level threshold ladder.

gauge.notes: Wisconsin River Trips ties the route to USGS 05418400 North Fork Maquoketa River near Fulton, IA, downstream on the same river. USGS also lists 05418100 at Dyersville, but this pass did not verify current discharge availability or route-specific bands for that station.

thresholdSupport.notes: Wisconsin River Trips publishes a route-specific Fulton-gauge ladder for the Dyersville route: less than 150 cfs perhaps too shallow, 151-230 cfs very shallow, 231-300 cfs average and good, 301-400 cfs above average/good target, 401-550 cfs still acceptable, 551-700 cfs high and likely unattractive, 701-1000 cfs very high/advanced, and 1001+ cfs expert-only.

endpoints.putIn: Candy Cane Park / Dyersville riverwalk area
endpoints.takeOut: Rockville Road bridge access
endpoints.coordinateSource: WRT names Candy Cane Park as the put-in and describes public riverwalk context in Dyersville. The Rockville Road take-out was not accepted because the route report describes a poor muddy bridge access and this pass did not find a land-manager page, official access table, or named map feature confirming public canoe/kayak access there.

fresh_source_discovery.recommendedNextStep: Use bounded internet research to surface a genuinely new official route, access, threshold, or gauge source family before spending more time on this candidate.
